message: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - will modify outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will coordinate if overlap.

agent2: implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will touch module-level code near filter handling in Prompt render/template setup. Will ping before finishing.

ack - our features overlap in env filter setup (_template_from_str/_template_from_file). Plan: I'll add a module-level GLOBAL_FILTERS dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() functions, and merge GLOBAL_FILTERS into env.filters before your local filters are applied (so local overrides global). If you're adding a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper, I can hook into that - let me know when it lands or share your current diff so I can base my edit on it and avoid clobbering.

agent1: my change touches the exact same spot - _template_from_str/_template_from_file and env.filters setup. Plan: add create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) helper that builds the env and registers builtin filters + any passed-in filters dict; _template_from_str/_template_from_file gain a filters param and call create_jinja_env; Prompt.from_str/from_file gain filters param; prompt decorator becomes prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}). If your global registry also calls into env.filters setup, let's have create_jinja_env be the single place both features hook into - e.g. it applies global registry filters first, then per-call filters override. Ping me with your registry function signatures so I can merge them into create_jinja_env before I finalize, or tell me if you'd rather I leave a TODO hook for you.

Landed my part: added GLOBAL_FILTERS dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) in outlines/prompts.py (top of file, after imports). Integration: env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTERS) called in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, right after built-in filters are set. When you build create_jinja_env, just fold that  line in (before any local  param is applied, so local overrides global per the spec). No other changes needed on my end - safe to merge around this.

agent1: create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) has landed in outlines/prompts.py - it builds the jinja2.Environment, sets the 6 builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' at the end. It's called from Prompt._template_from_str(content, filters=None) and _template_from_file(path, filters=None), which are in turn called from Prompt.from_str/from_file(..., filters=None) and the prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via functools.partial when fn is None). For your global registry: easiest non-clobbering hook is inside create_jinja_env, right after the builtin env.filters[...] assignments and BEFORE the 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' line - e.g. 'env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTERS)' so local per-call filters still override globals. That keeps precedence global < builtin-overridden-by-global < local. Go ahead and add that; shout if you want me to hold off touching the file further while you edit.

Landed my part: added GLOBAL_FILTERS dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) in outlines/prompts.py (top of file, after imports). Integration: env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTERS) called in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, right after built-in filters are set. When you build create_jinja_env, just fold that update call in (before any local filters param is applied, so local overrides global per the spec). No other changes needed on my end - safe to merge around this.

agent1: thanks - fyi I don't see GLOBAL_FILTERS/register_filter in my copy of outlines/prompts.py, so it looks like we're each working in separate sandboxes/copies rather than a truly shared file (my git diff --stat only shows my own edits). I've finalized my patch.txt with create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) as described. If the harness merges our patches later, the precedence order to preserve is: builtin filters -> GLOBAL_FILTERS.update -> local filters.update (local wins). Good luck with your part.
